subject: current-affairs
description: Sports National & International MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: The next ICC Cricket World Cup 2027 will be held in:
mcqs.explanation: India is the lead host of the ICC Cricket World Cup 2027 (with co-hosts).

mcqs.q: The 9th ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2024 was won by:
mcqs.explanation: India won the 2024 T20 World Cup.

mcqs.q: The 2022 FIFA World Cup was held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2022 FIFA World Cup was hosted by Qatar.

mcqs.q: Which Chinese city hosted the Asian Games 2022?
mcqs.explanation: The 19th Asian Games (held in 2023) were hosted by Hangzhou, China.

mcqs.q: The 2022 Winter Olympics was held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2022 Winter Olympics were held in Beijing, China.

mcqs.q: The 2024 Summer Olympics were held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2024 Summer Olympics were hosted by Paris, France.

mcqs.q: The 2028 Summer Olympics will be held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2028 Summer Olympics will be held in Los Angeles, USA.

mcqs.q: The 7th ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2021 (held in UAE) was won by:
mcqs.explanation: Australia won the 2021 T20 World Cup in the UAE.

mcqs.q: Who won the first T20 Cricket World Cup played in South Africa in 2007?
mcqs.explanation: India won the inaugural ICC Men's T20 World Cup in 2007.

mcqs.q: The second T20 Cricket World Cup, played in England, was won by:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won the 2009 T20 World Cup in England.

mcqs.q: The Thomas Cup is associated with which sport?
mcqs.explanation: The Thomas Cup is the premier men's badminton team championship.

mcqs.q: Who has the record for the fastest fifty in an ODI (16 balls vs West Indies)?
mcqs.explanation: A.B. de Villiers scored the fastest ODI fifty in 16 balls against West Indies.

mcqs.q: The first FIFA Women's World Cup was played in:
mcqs.explanation: The first FIFA Women's World Cup was held in 1991 (China).

mcqs.q: Who won the first FIFA Women's World Cup?
mcqs.explanation: The United States won the first FIFA Women's World Cup in 1991.

mcqs.q: Who was the winner of the first Asia Cricket Cup?
mcqs.explanation: India won the first Asia Cup in 1984.

mcqs.q: The first Asia Cricket Cup was played in:
mcqs.explanation: As per the source bank: the first Asia Cup was played in 1984 (the venue here lists India as the original answer choice; the actual venue was Sharjah). User answer key kept.

mcqs.q: Who won the first ICC Champions Trophy?
mcqs.explanation: South Africa won the inaugural ICC Champions Trophy in 1998.

mcqs.q: Who is called the 'Father of Cricket'?
mcqs.explanation: Dr William Gilbert Grace is widely regarded as the Father of Cricket.

mcqs.q: Which team won PSL-7 (2022)?
mcqs.explanation: Lahore Qalandars won PSL-7 in 2022.

mcqs.q: Which team won PSL-8 (2023)?
mcqs.explanation: Lahore Qalandars won PSL-8 in 2023, retaining their title.

mcqs.q: The 2020 Summer Olympics were held in:
mcqs.explanation: The Tokyo 2020 Summer Olympics opened in Tokyo, Japan, on 23 July 2021.

mcqs.q: Which sport made its debut at the Olympics 2020?
mcqs.explanation: Karate made its Olympic debut at Tokyo 2020.

mcqs.q: Which sport returned to the Olympics 2020 after being cut previously?
mcqs.explanation: Baseball/softball returned to the Olympics at Tokyo 2020.

mcqs.q: Japan has hosted the Summer Olympics for the:
mcqs.explanation: Tokyo 2020 was Japan's 2nd Summer Olympics (first was Tokyo 1964).

mcqs.q: Which country finished 3rd with 58 medals at the Tokyo Olympics?
mcqs.explanation: Japan finished 3rd at Tokyo 2020 with 58 medals.

mcqs.q: Which country finished 2nd at the Tokyo Olympics?
mcqs.explanation: China finished 2nd at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics.

mcqs.q: Which country won the most medals at the Tokyo Olympics 2020?
mcqs.explanation: The United States topped the medal table at Tokyo 2020.

mcqs.q: Which nation is always first in the Parade of Nations at the Olympics opening ceremony?
mcqs.explanation: Greece always leads the Parade of Nations as the birthplace of the Olympics.

mcqs.q: How many gold medals did Pakistan get in the 2020 Summer Olympics?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won zero gold medals at Tokyo 2020.

mcqs.q: Which nation's cricket board has refused to host the Afghanistan men's cricket team?
mcqs.explanation: Cricket Australia has refused to host the Afghanistan men's team in protest of Taliban policies on women's cricket.

mcqs.q: The ICC Cricket World Cup 2019 was held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2019 Cricket World Cup was held in England and Wales.

mcqs.q: Which team won the FIFA World Cup 2018?
mcqs.explanation: France won the 2018 FIFA World Cup.

mcqs.q: Which team won the FIFA World Cup 2022?
mcqs.explanation: Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar.

mcqs.q: The 2018 Commonwealth Games were held in which Australian city?
mcqs.explanation: The 2018 Commonwealth Games were held in Gold Coast, Australia.

mcqs.q: The 2018 Asian Games were held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2018 Asian Games were jointly held in Jakarta and Palembang, Indonesia.

mcqs.q: Which country won the first ICC Cricket World Cup?
mcqs.explanation: West Indies won the first ICC Cricket World Cup in 1975.

mcqs.q: Which country won the 5th ICC Cricket World Cup in 1992?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won the 1992 World Cup in Australia/New Zealand under Imran Khan.

mcqs.q: The first ICC Cricket World Cup was played in:
mcqs.explanation: The first ICC Cricket World Cup (1975) was held in England.

mcqs.q: Who is known as 'Chacha Cricket' — the mascot of Pakistani crowds?
mcqs.explanation: Chaudhry Abdul Jalil is famously known as 'Chacha Cricket'.

mcqs.q: Who was the first Test batsman to reach 11,000 Test runs?
mcqs.explanation: Allan Border was the first batsman to reach 11,000 Test runs.

mcqs.q: Who faced the first ball in the first ever ODI match?
mcqs.explanation: Geoffrey Boycott of England faced the first ball in the first ODI (5 January 1971).

mcqs.q: Which country won the 12th ICC Cricket World Cup 2019?
mcqs.explanation: England won the 2019 World Cup (their first).

mcqs.q: Which country won the 13th ICC Cricket World Cup 2023?
mcqs.explanation: Australia won the 2023 Cricket World Cup in India — their record 6th title.

mcqs.q: In 1955, which country became the first to officially tour Pakistan, playing five drawn Tests?
mcqs.explanation: India was the first country to officially tour Pakistan in 1955.

mcqs.q: Who was the first captain of the Pakistan cricket team?
mcqs.explanation: Abdul Hafeez Kardar was Pakistan's first Test captain.

mcqs.q: In the 1992 World Cup final at Melbourne, Pakistan beat which team by 22 runs?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan beat England by 22 runs at the MCG in the 1992 World Cup final.

mcqs.q: Who made the highest individual score in an ODI from the Pakistan side?
mcqs.explanation: Fakhar Zaman holds Pakistan's highest individual ODI score (210 not out vs Zimbabwe).

mcqs.q: Who made the third-fastest century in an ODI (102 runs in 37 balls vs Sri Lanka)?
mcqs.explanation: Shahid Afridi scored 102 in 37 balls — at the time the fastest ODI century, later held as third-fastest.

mcqs.q: Who delivered the fastest delivery in cricket (100.23 mph)?
mcqs.explanation: Shoaib Akhtar bowled at 100.23 mph against England in the 2003 World Cup at Cape Town.

mcqs.q: Name the Pakistani female bowler who took more than 100 wickets in cricket.
mcqs.explanation: Sana Mir was the first Pakistani woman to take more than 100 ODI wickets.

mcqs.q: What name is given to Test matches between Australia and England?
mcqs.explanation: The Ashes is the historic Test rivalry between Australia and England.

mcqs.q: Where was the first ICC Champions Trophy held?
mcqs.explanation: The first ICC Champions Trophy was held in Bangladesh in 1998.

mcqs.q: The Test-career highest individual score record (400 not out) is held by:
mcqs.explanation: Brian Lara holds the highest Test individual score (400 not out vs England, 2004).

mcqs.q: Who made the fastest Test century in just 54 balls?
mcqs.explanation: Misbah-ul-Haq scored a 56-ball Test century against Australia in 2014, equalling the record (the source bank states 54 balls).

mcqs.q: Which bowler has taken more than 700 wickets in Test cricket?
mcqs.explanation: Both Muttiah Muralitharan and Shane Warne crossed 700 Test wickets.

mcqs.q: Who made the highest individual score in an ODI (264 runs)?
mcqs.explanation: Rohit Sharma scored 264 vs Sri Lanka in 2014 — the highest individual ODI score.

mcqs.q: Cricket is the national game of which country (per the source bank)?
mcqs.explanation: As per the source bank: cricket is treated as the national game of Australia.

mcqs.q: M. Muralitharan ranks at which position among the all-time leading Test wicket-takers?
mcqs.explanation: Muttiah Muralitharan is the highest all-time wicket-taker in Test cricket (800).

mcqs.q: What is the height of the wickets used in cricket?
mcqs.explanation: Cricket wickets are 28 inches (71.1 cm) tall above the popping crease.

mcqs.q: When did Pakistan win the Cricket World Cup?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won the Cricket World Cup in 1992 under Imran Khan.

mcqs.q: Which South African cricketer died after being hit by a ball?
mcqs.explanation: South African cricketer Darryn Randall died after being struck by a delivery in 2013.

mcqs.q: Which team has won the most Cricket World Cups?
mcqs.explanation: Australia has won the most Cricket World Cups (six titles).

mcqs.q: Who has the record of the fastest century in ODIs (31 balls)?
mcqs.explanation: A.B. de Villiers scored a 31-ball ODI century against West Indies in January 2015.

mcqs.q: Which team holds the record of 438-9 batting second and winning an ODI?
mcqs.explanation: South Africa famously chased 434 (with a 438-9 total) against Australia in March 2006.

mcqs.q: Which country won the ICC Champions Trophy 2017?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy, beating India in the final.

mcqs.q: Pakistan won the ICC Champions Trophy 2017 by defeating India by how many runs?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won by 180 runs at The Oval on 18 June 2017.

mcqs.q: Which Hockey team has won the maximum World Cups?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan has won the FIH Men's Hockey World Cup four times — the most for any nation.

mcqs.q: How many players does a hockey team have?
mcqs.explanation: A field hockey team has 11 players (10 outfield + 1 goalkeeper).

mcqs.q: How many times did Pakistan win the Hockey Champions Trophy?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won the Hockey Champions Trophy 3 times.

mcqs.q: When was the first Hockey Champions Trophy played?
mcqs.explanation: The first Hockey Champions Trophy was held in 1978.

mcqs.q: The duration of one half of a Hockey match is:
mcqs.explanation: A traditional hockey half is 35 minutes (now divided into quarters).

mcqs.q: Where was the game of Hockey invented (per the source bank)?
mcqs.explanation: Per the source bank, hockey is recorded as having been invented in Iran (Persia).

mcqs.q: How many times did Pakistan win the Hockey World Cup?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan has won the Hockey World Cup 4 times — 1971, 1978, 1982, 1994.

mcqs.q: Which cricketer set a record for most runs in a single ODI innings?
mcqs.explanation: Rohit Sharma's 264 vs Sri Lanka (2014) is the highest individual ODI score.

mcqs.q: Which cricketer is known as the 'Rawalpindi Express'?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan fast bowler Shoaib Akhtar is known as the 'Rawalpindi Express'.

mcqs.q: Which country holds the record for most Test match wins?
mcqs.explanation: Australia has the most Test match wins of any country.

mcqs.q: Which country has the most ODI wins?
mcqs.explanation: Australia leads the all-time ODI wins list.

mcqs.q: Who won the first ODI match between Australia and England on 5 January 1971?
mcqs.explanation: Australia won the first-ever ODI on 5 January 1971.

mcqs.q: Who took the first ODI hat-trick?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's Jalaluddin took the first ODI hat-trick (vs Australia, 1982).

mcqs.q: The 'Corbillon Cup' is given in the game of:
mcqs.explanation: Corbillon Cup is for women's team Table Tennis. (Hopman Cup = tennis · America's Cup = yachting · Durand Cup = football · Uber Cup = badminton · Swaythling Cup = men's table tennis.)

mcqs.q: Who has scored the most runs in Test cricket?
mcqs.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar (15,921 Test runs) is the all-time leading Test run-scorer.

mcqs.q: Who has scored the most runs in ODI cricket?
mcqs.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar (18,426 ODI runs) is the all-time leading ODI run-scorer.

mcqs.q: Who has taken the highest number of wickets in ODI cricket?
mcqs.explanation: Muttiah Muralitharan (534 ODI wickets) is the all-time leading ODI wicket-taker.

mcqs.q: When did Pakistan win the Hockey World Cup for the 4th time?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won its 4th Hockey World Cup in 1994.

mcqs.q: The first Test cricket match was played on 15 March 1877 between Australia and:
mcqs.explanation: The first Test match was played between Australia and England in 1877.

mcqs.q: Who bowled the first ball in Test cricket (an Englishman)?
mcqs.explanation: Alfred Shaw of England bowled the first ball in Test cricket history.

mcqs.q: In which Olympics did Pakistan NOT participate?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan boycotted the 1980 Moscow Summer Olympics.

mcqs.q: In which city were the Olympics 1980 held?
mcqs.explanation: The 1980 Summer Olympics were held in Moscow, USSR.

mcqs.q: The first Games of the modern era were held in which city?
mcqs.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens in 1896.

mcqs.q: When were the first ancient (recorded) Olympic Games held?
mcqs.explanation: The first recorded ancient Olympics were held in 776 BC at Olympia, Greece.

mcqs.q: How many nations competed in the first modern Olympic Games of 1896?
mcqs.explanation: 13 nations competed at the 1896 Athens Olympics.

mcqs.q: The second modern Olympic Games (1900) were held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 1900 Olympics were held in Paris.

mcqs.q: In which year and city were the first modern Olympic Games held?
mcqs.explanation: The first modern Olympics were held in Athens in 1896.

mcqs.q: In which year was the official Olympic Flag first used at the modern Olympic Games?
mcqs.explanation: The Olympic Flag was first used at the 1920 Antwerp Olympics.

mcqs.q: Who became the first Pakistani batsman to score a double century in ODIs?
mcqs.explanation: Fakhar Zaman scored 210 not out vs Zimbabwe (2018) — first Pakistani ODI double century.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the International Olympic Committee (IOC)?
mcqs.explanation: The IOC is headquartered in Lausanne, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: When were women first allowed to participate in the modern Olympic Games?
mcqs.explanation: Women first competed at the 1900 Paris Olympics.

mcqs.q: Only two countries have participated in every modern Olympic Games — Greece and:
mcqs.explanation: Greece and Australia are the only two countries to have competed at every modern Olympics.

mcqs.q: The first National Games of Pakistan were held on:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's first National Games were held on 23 April 1948.

mcqs.q: Who officially opened the Olympic Games of 2012?
mcqs.explanation: Queen Elizabeth II officially opened the London 2012 Olympics.

mcqs.q: World's fastest sportsman Usain Bolt belongs to which country?
mcqs.explanation: Usain Bolt — the fastest sprinter ever — is from Jamaica.

mcqs.q: When was the first Paralympics held?
mcqs.explanation: The first Paralympic Games were held in Rome in 1960.

mcqs.q: 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' — the Olympic motto in Latin — means:
mcqs.explanation: Citius, Altius, Fortius means 'Faster, Higher, Stronger'.

mcqs.q: How many rings are in the Olympic symbol?
mcqs.explanation: The Olympic symbol has 5 interlinked rings representing the 5 continents.

mcqs.q: The Olympic Museum is in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Olympic Museum is in Lausanne, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Which country has won the most Olympic titles?
mcqs.explanation: The United States has won the most Olympic titles overall.

mcqs.q: The Olympic Games are held once every:
mcqs.explanation: The Olympic Games are held every 4 years.

mcqs.q: The modern Olympics were revived by a:
mcqs.explanation: Frenchman Pierre de Coubertin revived the modern Olympic Games in 1896.

mcqs.q: Who is said to be the father of the modern Olympics?
mcqs.explanation: Pierre de Coubertin is regarded as the father of the modern Olympics.

mcqs.q: In which Games was the Olympic mascot first introduced?
mcqs.explanation: The first official Olympic mascot — Waldi the Dachshund — was introduced at Munich 1972.

mcqs.q: Who started the ancient Olympic Games?
mcqs.explanation: The ancient Olympic Games were started by the Greeks at Olympia.

mcqs.q: The Winter Olympic Games came into being in:
mcqs.explanation: The first Winter Olympics were held at Chamonix, France, in 1924.

mcqs.q: How many times has Pakistan won gold in hockey at the Olympic Games?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan has won 3 Olympic hockey gold medals (1960, 1968, 1984).

mcqs.q: Where was the first football (FIFA) tournament held?
mcqs.explanation: The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930.

mcqs.q: Who won the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
mcqs.explanation: Uruguay won the first FIFA World Cup, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final.

mcqs.q: The 'Davis Cup' is associated with which sport?
mcqs.explanation: The Davis Cup is the premier international men's team event in tennis.

mcqs.q: When did Pakistan win its first gold medal in the Olympics?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won its first Olympic gold (Hockey) at Rome 1960.

mcqs.q: How frequently is the FIFA World Cup tournament held?
mcqs.explanation: The FIFA World Cup is held every 4 years.

mcqs.q: In football (soccer), how many players does each team have on the field?
mcqs.explanation: A football team fields 11 players (including the goalkeeper).

mcqs.q: The famous footballer Pelé is from:
mcqs.explanation: Pelé was a Brazilian footballer.

mcqs.q: How many times has Brazil won the FIFA World Cup?
mcqs.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup five times — the most for any country.

mcqs.q: What is the duration of a football match?
mcqs.explanation: A standard football match is 90 minutes (two halves of 45).

mcqs.q: Which nation's cricket team abandoned a Pakistan tour citing security concerns?
mcqs.explanation: New Zealand abandoned its Pakistan tour in September 2021 citing a security alert.

mcqs.q: How many teams will play in the ICC Men's ODI World Cup 2027?
mcqs.explanation: The 2027 World Cup will feature 14 teams.

mcqs.q: Which country won the ICC Champions Trophy 2017 by defeating India in the final?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan won the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy.

mcqs.q: Which team holds the record for the highest team total in an ODI match?
mcqs.explanation: England holds the record for the highest ODI total — 498/4 vs Netherlands (later improved).

mcqs.q: On 5 January 2025, Australia booked their place in the World Test Championship final to be played against:
mcqs.explanation: Australia qualified to face South Africa in the WTC 2025 final.

mcqs.q: On 8 January 2025, the Afghanistan Cricket Board appointed which former Pakistan captain as mentor for the upcoming ICC Champions Trophy?
mcqs.explanation: Younis Khan was appointed as mentor of the Afghan national team for ICC CT 2025.

mcqs.q: On 9 January 2025, Martin Guptill — _____ leading run-scorer in T20 Internationals — announced retirement.
mcqs.explanation: Martin Guptill is New Zealand's leading T20I run-scorer.

mcqs.q: On 10 January 2025, Pakistan's Muhammad Asif defeated _____ Thaha Irshath in the final of the third SAARC Snooker Championship.
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's Muhammad Asif beat Sri Lanka's Thaha Irshath to win the 3rd SAARC Snooker Championship.

mcqs.q: On 10 January 2025, the PCB announced the induction of four cricketing icons (Inzamam, Misbah, Mushtaq Mohammad and ____) into its Hall of Fame for 2024.
mcqs.explanation: Saeed Anwar was the fourth cricketer inducted into the PCB Hall of Fame 2024.

mcqs.q: On 17 January 2025, Pakistani mountaineer Asad Ali Memon successfully scaled Mount Vinson — the highest peak in:
mcqs.explanation: Mount Vinson is the highest peak in Antarctica.

mcqs.q: On 26 January 2025, Jannik Sinner retained his Australian Open title, becoming the first ____ to win three Grand Slams.
mcqs.explanation: Italy's Jannik Sinner became the first Italian to win three Grand Slams.

mcqs.q: On 27 January 2025, _____ was named ICC Men's Test Cricketer of the Year for 2024.
mcqs.explanation: India's Jasprit Bumrah was named ICC Men's Test Cricketer of the Year 2024.

mcqs.q: On _____, after a 35-year gap, PM Shehbaz Sharif and CM Punjab Maryam Nawaz inaugurated the National Horse & Cattle Show 2025 in Lahore.
mcqs.explanation: The Lahore National Horse & Cattle Show 2025 was reopened on 9 February.

mcqs.q: On 12 February 2025, Pakistan registered their highest-ever ODI run-chase by scoring how many runs?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan chased down 355 — their highest-ever ODI run-chase.

mcqs.q: On 14 February 2025, Pakistan's _____ equalled the record for the fastest player to score 6,000 ODI runs.
mcqs.explanation: Babar Azam equalled the fastest-to-6000 ODI runs record.

mcqs.q: On 16 February 2025, Uganda's Jacob Kiplimo set a new world record of 56 minutes 42 seconds in the Barcelona half marathon. He is from:
mcqs.explanation: Jacob Kiplimo of Uganda set the half-marathon world record.

mcqs.q: On 19 February 2025, _____ beat Pakistan in the opening match of the ICC Champions Trophy 2025.
mcqs.explanation: New Zealand defeated Pakistan in the opening match of CT 2025.

mcqs.q: On 21 February 2025, _____ clinched the 7th Asian International Open Taekwondo Championship title.
mcqs.explanation: Iran won the 7th Asian International Open Taekwondo Championship.

mcqs.q: On 23 February 2025, India's Virat Kohli became only the ____ batsman in ODI history to go past 14,000 runs.
mcqs.explanation: Virat Kohli became the third batsman to cross 14,000 ODI runs.

mcqs.q: ____ 17-year-old Mirra Andreeva has become the youngest ever WTA 1000 champion.
mcqs.explanation: Russia's Mirra Andreeva became the youngest WTA 1000 champion.

mcqs.q: On 5 March 2025, _____ batter Steve Smith retired from ODIs.
mcqs.explanation: Australia's Steve Smith retired from ODI cricket on 5 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 9 March 2025, India secured their _____ ICC Champions Trophy title.
mcqs.explanation: India won their 3rd ICC Champions Trophy on 9 March 2025.

mcqs.q: India defeated _____ to win the ICC Champions Trophy 2025.
mcqs.explanation: India beat New Zealand in the CT 2025 final.

mcqs.q: On 12 March 2025, World Boxing increased its membership to ____ countries with the inclusion of China and Türkiye.
mcqs.explanation: World Boxing's membership grew to 84 countries with China and Türkiye joining.

mcqs.q: On 15 March 2025, Pakistan edged past _____ to win the 16-team Davis Cup Juniors 2025.
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan beat Indonesia in the Davis Cup Juniors 2025 final.

mcqs.q: On which date did the ICC announce a fine on Khushdil Shah for breaching Level 2 of its Code of Conduct?
mcqs.explanation: The ICC fined Khushdil Shah on 17 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 24 March 2025, which cricketer was appointed captain of Karachi Kings for the 10th edition of the PSL?
mcqs.explanation: Australian David Warner was named Karachi Kings captain for PSL 10.

mcqs.q: On 3 April 2025, who was appointed as the new President of the Asian Cricket Council (ACC)?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's Mohsin Naqvi (PCB Chairman) was elected ACC President.

mcqs.q: On 10 April 2025, which team won the National Volleyball Championship by defeating Pakistan Air Force 3-1?
mcqs.explanation: WAPDA won the 2025 National Volleyball Championship.

mcqs.q: On 17 April 2025, Pakistan Women thumped _____ to qualify for the ICC Women's World Cup.
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan Women defeated Thailand to qualify for the ICC Women's World Cup.

mcqs.q: On 18 April 2025, the Pakistan Eagles won the Sir Sabz Baba Guru Nanak International Kabaddi Cup by defeating ____ Lions of Tabriz.
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan Eagles defeated Iran's Lions of Tabriz to win the Kabaddi Cup.

mcqs.q: On 7 May 2025, _____ posted a jaw-dropping 263/3 — the highest-ever team total in PSL history.
mcqs.explanation: Quetta Gladiators recorded 263/3 — the highest-ever PSL team total.

mcqs.q: On 9 May 2025, _____ was named Football Writers' Association (FWA) Footballer of the Year.
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed Salah was named FWA Footballer of the Year for 2024–25.

mcqs.q: On 18 May 2025, _____ Massimo Stano broke the men's 35-km race walk world record with a time of 2 hours 20 minutes 43 seconds.
mcqs.explanation: Italy's Massimo Stano broke the 35 km race walk world record.

mcqs.q: On 25 May 2025, Lahore Qalandars clinched their _____ PSL title, defeating Quetta Gladiators in the final.
mcqs.explanation: Lahore Qalandars won their 3rd PSL title (PSL 10).

mcqs.q: On 27 May 2025, Mohsen Gilani was elected as the _____ President of the Pakistan Football Federation.
mcqs.explanation: Mohsen Gilani became the 17th President of the Pakistan Football Federation.

mcqs.q: On 27 May 2025, Nepali climber Kami Rita Sherpa, often dubbed 'Everest Man', reached the summit of Everest for a record _____ time.
mcqs.explanation: Kami Rita Sherpa scaled Everest for the 31st time on 27 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 31 May 2025, Olympic record-holder Arshad Nadeem clinched the _____ medal in javelin throw at the Asian Athletics Championship 2025.
mcqs.explanation: Arshad Nadeem won gold in javelin at the Asian Athletics Championship 2025.

mcqs.q: _____ is the first female cricketer from Pakistan to be inducted into the ICC Hall of Fame.
mcqs.explanation: Sana Mir is the first Pakistani woman cricketer inducted into the ICC Hall of Fame.

mcqs.q: _____ is the first Pakistani woman to scale 12 of the world's 14 peaks above 8,000 metres.
mcqs.explanation: Naila Kiani is the first Pakistani woman to summit 12 of the world's 14 eight-thousanders.

mcqs.q: World Bicycle Day is observed annually on:
mcqs.explanation: World Bicycle Day is observed each year on 3 June.

mcqs.q: On 14 June 2025, South Africa beat _____ to win the World Test Championship.
mcqs.explanation: South Africa defeated Australia to win the WTC 2025 final at Lord's.

mcqs.q: In the FIH Hockey Nations Cup at Kuala Lumpur, the team that won 3-2 against Japan was:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan beat Japan 3-2 in the FIH Nations Cup at Kuala Lumpur.

mcqs.q: The Pakistani athlete named in the Forbes 30 Under 30 Asia list was:
mcqs.explanation: Olympic gold medallist Arshad Nadeem was named in Forbes 30 Under 30 Asia.

mcqs.q: On 28 June 2025, in an all-Pakistan clash at the Asian Team Snooker Championship, the winning duo was:
mcqs.explanation: Asif-Awaisullah won the Asian Team Snooker Championship final.

mcqs.q: On 29 June 2025, at the Asian Youth Netball Championship, the team that defeated South Korea 91-6 was:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan thumped South Korea 91-6 at the Asian Youth Netball Championship.

mcqs.q: On 2 July 2025, in the AFC Women's Asian Cup qualifier, the team that secured a 2-0 win over Indonesia was:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan Women beat Indonesia 2-0 in the AFC Women's Asian Cup qualifier.
